differentials.name: Intraarticular / systemic steroid injections mnemonic:   DS6
differentials.raw: Intraarticular / systemic steroid injections mnemonic:   DS6

differentials.name: Neurotraumatic theory =  repetitive trauma with absence of normal protective sensory feedback
differentials.raw: Neurotraumatic theory =  repetitive trauma with absence of normal protective sensory feedback

differentials.name: Neurovascular theory =  absence of neural stimuli → loss of sympathetic tone resulting in vasodilatation and hyperemia, which promotes bone resorption + weakening of subchondral bone
differentials.raw: Neurovascular theory =  absence of neural stimuli → loss of sympathetic tone resulting in vasodilatation and hyperemia, which promotes bone resorption + weakening of subchondral bone

differentials.name: notable absence of osteosclerosis + osteophyte formation
differentials.raw: notable absence of osteosclerosis + osteophyte formation

differentials.name: osteosclerosis + osteophyte formation
differentials.raw: osteosclerosis + osteophyte formation (early, attaining enormous size)

differentials.name: swollen + warm joint with normal WBC count + ESR
differentials.raw: swollen + warm joint with normal WBC count + ESR (infection may coexist)

differentials.name: usually painless joint; pain at presentation (in ⅓) with decreased response to deep pain + proprioception
differentials.raw: usually painless joint; pain at presentation (in ⅓) with decreased response to deep pain + proprioception

differentials.name: joint changes frequently precede neurologic deficit
differentials.raw: joint changes frequently precede neurologic deficit

differentials.name: synovial fluid: frequently xanthochromic / bloody, lipid crystals
differentials.raw: synovial fluid: frequently xanthochromic / bloody, lipid crystals (from bone marrow)

context: Pathophysiology: loss of proprioception with sensory deficits arising in the spinal cord / peripheral nerves Pathology: (a)  atrophic pattern (most common): joint destruction, resorption of fragments (osteoclasts + macrophages remove bone + cartilage debris), dissolution / "amputation" of periarticular bones, joint effusion Associated with:   syringomyelia, peripheral nerve lesion, also in diabetes Location:   non-weight-bearing joints of upper extremity DDx:   surgical amputation, septic arthritis (b)  hypertrophic pattern (only sensory nerves affected): joint destruction, fragmentation of bo
